reforms.description: The Dutch National Growth Fund was launched, providing EUR 20 billion for projects in knowledge development, research and development, innovation, and infrastructure, with an independent committee assessing proposals.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to stimulate economic growth through targeted investments in innovation and infrastructure, which can enhance productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The first tranche of the National Growth Fund was allocated in April 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: The fund represents a significant structural investment initiative aimed at enhancing the Netherlands' innovation capacity and addressing critical infrastructure needs.
reforms.source_quote: The recently launched EUR 20 billion National Growth Fund subsidises projects in the areas of knowledge development, research and development, innovation and infrastructure.

reforms.description: The Personal Learning and Development Budget (STAP) is set to be implemented in 2022, providing a training subsidy of up to EUR 1,000 per year for adults to encourage lifelong learning and skill development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By funding individual training, the initiative aims to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby increasing productivity and employability.
reforms.status_evidence: The STAP initiative is to be implemented in 2022 with an initial annual funding of EUR 200 million.
reforms.importance_rationale: The STAP initiative represents a significant step towards enhancing adult education and training, addressing skill mismatches in the labor market.
reforms.source_quote: The Personal learning and development budget (STAP) is to be implemented in 2022 with an initial annual funding of EUR 200 million.

reforms.description: The Netherlands is investing considerable resources in frontier digital technologies, notably quantum computing and artificial intelligence, which received substantial funding through the first tranche of the National Growth Fund.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investment in frontier technologies is expected to enhance productivity and innovation capacity across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: Funding for quantum computing and AI was allocated through the National Growth Fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to advancing cutting-edge technologies that can drive future economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Netherlands is investing considerable resources in frontier digital technologies, notably quantum computing and artificial intelligence, which received substantial funding through the first tranche of the National Growth Fund.

reforms.description: The government is promoting private sector investment in high-capacity fixed broadband networks through guidelines for infrastructure sharing, aiming to enhance digital connectivity and support innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband infrastructure is essential for enhancing digital adoption, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is examining how to promote private sector investment in high-capacity fixed broadband networks.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it addresses a critical infrastructure need that supports broader digital transformation efforts in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government is examining how to promote private sector investment in high-capacity fixed broadband networks within the telecommunication bill.

reforms.description: The government has established a National Cyber Security Centre and a Roadmap for Digital Hardware and Software Security to enhance the digital security of ICT products and services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing digital security fosters trust in digital infrastructure, which is crucial for encouraging innovation and digital adoption.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has stepped up its digital security efforts and established a National Cyber Security Centre.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is important for establishing a secure digital environment, which is essential for the growth of digital services and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has stepped up its digital security efforts and has established a National Cyber Security Centre.

reforms.description: The Dutch government initiated public-private partnerships to support the digitalisation of SMEs, including the Smart Industry Field Labs programme and the Accelerating the Digitalisation of SMEs programme.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives aim to enhance the digital capabilities of SMEs, which can lead to increased productivity and competitiveness in the market.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has made progress in supporting the digitalisation of SMEs through various initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant effort to enhance the digital capabilities of SMEs, which are crucial for economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Dutch government has made some progress in this regard, amongst others initiating public-private partnerships to support digitalisation of SMEs.

reforms.description: The Dutch government announced the Growth Facility scheme for start-ups, which provides credit guarantees to support access to finance for innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By easing access to finance, the scheme aims to foster innovation and growth among start-ups, which are vital for economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has extended credit guarantees under the Growth Facility scheme for start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it directly addresses the financing challenges faced by start-ups, promoting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government has extended credit guarantees, its Growth Facility scheme for start-ups, and raised the budget for the SEED Capital scheme.

reforms.description: The Dutch government has extended R&D grants and tax incentives to support software development, recognizing its importance as a driver of innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for software development is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has actively supported software development through grants and tax incentives.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant enhancement of existing funding mechanisms aimed at boosting innovation in the software sector.
reforms.source_quote: Recognising the importance of software as a driver to innovation, the government has extended R&D grants and tax incentives to support software development over the past decade.

reforms.description: The Dutch government has concluded a Knowledge and Innovation Covenant 2020-23, committing EUR 3 billion per year for collaborative research and innovation, matched by EUR 2 billion from private funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This joint funding initiative is expected to enhance collaborative research efforts and drive innovation across key sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed substantial funding for collaborative research and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This covenant represents a structural change in funding for innovation, fostering collaboration between public and private sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The government has concluded a Knowledge and Innovation Covenant 2020-23 with public and private partners.

reforms.description: The Dutch authorities launched a EUR 20 billion National Growth Fund to boost productivity through investments in education, infrastructure, and R&D and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investments in R&D and innovation are expected to enhance productivity and long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The fund started operating in 2021 to support various sectors.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund represents a significant investment aimed at enhancing the overall productivity and innovation landscape in the Netherlands.
reforms.source_quote: Dutch authorities launched a EUR 20 billion National Growth Fund, which started operating in 2021, to boost productivity through investments in education, infrastructure, and R&D and innovation.

reforms.description: The Dutch government has committed EUR 70 million to strengthen universities' capacity in STEM fields to address the shortage of graduates in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the supply of skilled graduates in STEM fields is essential for enhancing innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed EUR 70 million to strengthen universities’ offer and capacity in STEM.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding is significant for enhancing the educational capacity in STEM, which is crucial for future economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has committed EUR 70 million to strengthen universities’ offer and capacity in this domain.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Social Affairs and Employment plans to provide EUR 48 million annually to stimulate ICT training and development among SMEs, addressing the skills gap in the digital economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing ICT skills in SMEs is expected to improve productivity and innovation capabilities, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry of Social Affairs and Employment stated its intention to provide EUR 48 million a year from 2020 for SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for improving the digital skills of SMEs, which are crucial for their competitiveness and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Social Affairs and Employment stated its intention to provide EUR 48 million a year from 2020 for SMEs to stimulate further training and development.

reforms.description: The Netherlands aims to expand part-time higher education pathways for ICT professionals to address the shortage of ICT graduates, involving closer collaboration with the private sector in curriculum design.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the number of qualified ICT professionals, the reform supports the digital economy and enhances productivity in various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy includes plans to expand part-time higher education pathways for ICT professionals.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical skill shortage in the ICT sector, which is essential for the Netherlands' digital transformation.
reforms.source_quote: Expand part-time higher education pathways for ICT professionals.

reforms.description: The Netherlands plans to increase direct support to SMEs to facilitate the adoption of digital tools, including business advisory services and testing facilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing SMEs' digital capabilities is expected to improve their productivity and competitiveness in the market.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy includes measures to increase direct support to SMEs for digital adoption.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets a large segment of the economy, aiming to enhance digital adoption among SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: Increase direct support to SMEs to facilitate the adoption of digital tools, including business advisory services and testing facilities.
